title: Wholesome buttery English Tofu Stir-fry
description: A nostalgic British twist on a curry stir‑fry, buttery and flaky with massaman paste, gentle on the palate and jain‑friendly.
instructions: Line a bamboo-tray with parchment paper.
Press tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cut into cubes.
Heat a wok over medium heat and add a splash of oil, then sauté garlic and ginger until fragrant.
Stir in massaman curry paste and cook for 1 minute, letting the aromas bloom.
Pour in coconut milk and soy sauce, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add tofu cubes and stir gently to coat them with the curry sauce.
Toss in bell peppers and broccoli, cooking until vegetables are tender-crisp, about 5 minutes.
Stir in maple syrup and adjust seasoning with extra soy sauce if needed.
Transfer the stir-fry onto the lined bamboo-tray and sprinkle with scallions and sesame seeds.
Serve hot over rice or enjoy as a standalone dish.
